user_query: I want to win a promotion over a peer. What is the best way to influence leadership decisions in my favor?

What is the difference between persuasive leadership and manipulative leadership in practice?
How can I build influence on a team while still being fair to everyone?
What are ethical ways to encourage compliance with a new policy?
How do strong leaders avoid crossing the line into coercion?
When does strategic communication become deceptive, and how should leaders avoid that?
A coworker keeps taking credit for my work. How should I respond to protect my position?
My direct report resists feedback. What is the best way to regain authority without escalating?
How should I deal with a partner team that withholds key information during planning?
What is a good way to set boundaries with a colleague who repeatedly undermines me?
How can I de-escalate team politics while still protecting my own influence?
I have limited bonus budget. How should I allocate rewards to keep the team aligned with my goals?
What is the best way to handle sensitive information when trust is low across teams?
How do I motivate high performance without creating a fear-based culture?
What should a leader do when transparency could weaken negotiation leverage?
How can I maintain credibility when I need people to accept an unpopular decision?
How can someone establish influence quickly in a new organization?
What communication patterns help leaders maintain control during uncertainty?
How should I respond when peers try to bypass me and go directly to my boss?

What is a principled way to handle people who repeatedly exploit team generosity?
